key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct non-linear, history-dependent local stress fields by learning both loading history and spatial equilibrium.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate field prediction for elasto-plastic microstructures, delivering major speedups while staying adaptable across mesh resolutions and element types.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain history into a compact path-dependent state.
Physics-informed GNN reconstructs spatial stress fields from mesh connectivity and hidden state.
Training balances reconstruction and discrete-divergence equilibrium losses with linear warm-up.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a plate-with-a-hole microstructure, the model achieved about three orders of magnitude speedup over FEM and 1.9% cumulative error when tested on sequences twice as long as training.
watch_item: Generalization depends on the quality of mesh-connectivity graphs and the equilibrium penalty schedule, so unstable training or strongly different physics may require retuning.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
